summary: Auto-promote LLM-tagged staged_tags + rework human review as editorial overlay (RAG signal pipeline)

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Two coupled changes per docs/autopromote/design.md §11: Ticket A (auto_promote.py tool) ships RAG signal at tier=proposed/inferred without human review; Ticket B (companion) reworks the human review path so Accept always writes verified, and Reject/Reassign DELETE the auto-promoted edges they're correcting. Without B, A's edges can't be retracted by the operator; B-first is the safer landing order. Live DB post-dedupe baseline 2026-04-26: 14296 qwen-pass pending tags (2082 score=3, 7622 score=2, 4592 score=1), 751 Carnice-9b pending (excluded by default --model filter). Auto-promote at default --score 3 should write ~2082 EXPRESSES rows at tier=proposed.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Operator safety constraint (per user directive): NO --apply against data/guru.db until: (1) C1+C2 land and parity harness green, (2) tests/test_auto_promote.py green, (3) C5 confirms --dry-run output matches design's predicted ~2082 score=3 rows, (4) a separate snapshot is taken at ~/guru-backups/guru-<ts>-pre-autopromote.db before any production --apply. The auto_promote.sh wrapper's built-in snapshot is the second safety net, but the operator-initiated pre-test snapshot is the first. C5 is explicitly NOT 'apply to live'; it's 'sanity-check the script against a copy of live'. Production --apply is a separate operator-driven step after the parent ticket merges to main.

resolution.note: Auto-promote + editorial-overlay rework complete on todo/709e9d02. Tier semantics now: verified=human-reviewed only (Accept), proposed=score>=2 auto OR score=1 human, inferred=BELONGS_TO OR score=1 auto. CLI + web review tools mirror each other (parity green). Reject/Reassign retract auto-promoted edges. auto_promote.py + .sh ship; live --dry-run shows 2,062 score=3 candidates; shadow --apply validated; live DB pristine. 83 unit + parity harness green. Operator can run scripts/auto_promote.sh --apply for the production write whenever ready (built-in snapshot + integrity_check + manifest).
